Transformer Architecture Review: The Transformer has become the dominant architecture in NLP, computer vision, and multimodal AI since its introduction in 2017. Key components include multi-head self-attention, position-wise feedforward networks, residual connections, and layer normalization, all of which contribute to stable training at scale. Variants such as encoder-only (BERT), decoder-only (GPT), and encoder-decoder (T5, BART) models are suited to different tasks — encoding for understanding, decoding for generation, and encoder-decoder for sequence-to-sequence tasks. Scaling laws demonstrate that model performance improves predictably with increases in model parameters, training data, and compute, motivating the development of ever-larger models.
